# backend/routers/agent_platform.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-11T10:01:43Z

## Purpose
Unified agent platform setup/status API.

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `typing` import Any
- from `fastapi` import APIRouter, Depends, Query, Request
- from `core.middleware` import current_tenant_id
- from `schemas.agent_platform` import AgentPlatformManifestResponse, AgentPlatformStatusResponse, AgentPolicyEvaluationRequest, AgentPolicyEvaluationResponse, ApprovalBrokerRequest
- from `services.agent_platform_service` import AgentPlatformIntegrationService

## Inputs
(no CLI / env inputs — library module or implicit)

## Process
### Functions
- `get_agent_platform_service()`: Dependency provider for unified agent platform integrations.
- `get_status(service)`: Return setup/runtime status for Harness, OpenClaw, Paperclip, PoliysAI, CUA, and Stagehand.
- `get_manifest(service)`: Return the unified agent platform integration contract.
- `evaluate_governance(request, service)`: Evaluate whether one agent/tool action is allowed.
- `execute_cua(http_request, request, service)`: Run a policy-gated CUA/browser action envelope. Defaults to dry-run.
- `decide_approval_broker(http_request, request, service)`: Classify approve/submit/next requests and optionally enqueue safe next work.
- `run_typed_council(http_request, request, service)`: Run the opt-in Pydantic AI author/reviewer/chair council.
- `list_cua_audit(http_request, limit, since_ts, service)`: Read back the CUA audit log, tenant-scoped from the X-Tenant-ID header.
- `get_adapters_status(http_request, service)`: Aggregated snapshot of all §56 Stage-1 adapters.
- `get_tenant_activity(http_request, limit, since_ts, service)`: Unified per-tenant activity feed across CUA + admin + Paperclip.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- Pydantic v2: model has model_copy with update; mutate via metadata dict.
- Merge Idempotency-Key header into request if body didn't supply one.
- Body field wins over header (more explicit; matches user intent).
